title: Welcome to the Agency
briefing: Detective. Before we put you on a real case, let us show you how we operate around here. This is a practice run. No pressure — we just want to make sure you know your tools.

evidence.html:
Messages
Unknown Number
Hi grandma! It's Tommy. I lost my phone and borrowed a friend's. Can you call me back?
Tommy? Is this really you?
Yes grandma! Please don't tell mom and dad yet. I need help urgently.

hotspots.label: Unknown phone number
hotspots.explanation: The message came from a number not saved in any contacts. Your real grandson's number would already be in your phone.
hotspots.tutorial_hint: Tap the top of the message where it says 'Unknown Number' — that is your first clue.

hotspots.label: Secrecy request
hotspots.explanation: Asking you to keep a secret from family is a classic pressure tactic. Real family emergencies do not require secrecy.
hotspots.tutorial_hint: Now tap the last message — what is the sender asking you to keep secret?

deduction_options.text: This looks suspicious — unknown number and secrecy request are warning signs
This is probably my grandson — I should respond right away

learning_summary: Always verify by calling your grandchild on their real, saved number before responding to messages from unknown numbers.
